violations.finding: Bare `python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` call at line 151 without `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate &&` prefix. The call creates a fake agent-scores.json fixture for test (d) without venv activation.
violations.recommendation: Replace bare `python3` with `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` ... `PYEOF )` to match the compliant pattern used at line 86 of the same file.

violations.finding: Bare `python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` call at line 194 without `source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate &&` prefix. The call creates a fake agent-scores.json fixture for test (e) without venv activation.
violations.recommendation: Replace bare `python3` with `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` ... `PYEOF )` to match the compliant pattern used at line 86 of the same file.

violations.finding: Pre-existing Chinese characters in agent prompt: "行动 (act), not just 读 (read)" in a prompt prose paragraph explaining why four INJECTION_PROOF fields are required. Not exempt under the mascot-rank-labels exemption (those are 见习学徒/初级工匠 etc.) or the verbatim-quote exemption (restricted to docs/dev/ticket-*.md). This is a real Standard 6 violation.
violations.recommendation: Rewrite in English: replace the inline Chinese parentheticals with English: e.g. "proves genuine action (not mere reading)".
violations.advisory_note: Pre-existing in HEAD and HEAD~1; not introduced by task 20260525-050824 diff. Under AC-2.6(b) this finding is advisory/non-blocking for this cycle.

violations.finding: Pre-existing Chinese characters in agent prompt: "行动 (act), not just 读 (read)" — same text as qa.md:1297, present in a prompt prose paragraph. Not exempt under any documented exemption clause.
violations.recommendation: Rewrite in English: replace the inline Chinese parentheticals with English: e.g. "proves genuine action (not mere reading)".
violations.advisory_note: Pre-existing in HEAD and HEAD~1; not introduced by task 20260525-050824 diff. Under AC-2.6(b) this finding is advisory/non-blocking for this cycle.

violations.finding: Pre-existing Chinese characters in agent prompt: "行动 (act), not just 读 (read)" — same pattern as qa.md and dev.md. Not in the changed-files list for this cycle.
violations.recommendation: Rewrite in English: replace the inline Chinese parentheticals with English: e.g. "proves genuine action (not mere reading)".
violations.advisory_note: ba.md is not in the --changed-files list for this task. Pre-existing violation; advisory/non-blocking under AC-2.6(b).

violations.finding: Pre-existing Chinese text: "自我总结的内容质量靠 orchestrator 即兴生成：无所谓" — a verbatim user quote embedded in orchestrator instructions inside commands/close.md. The verbatim-quote exemption applies only to docs/dev/ticket-*.md, not to commands/*.md files.
violations.recommendation: Move the verbatim Chinese user quote to docs/dev/ticket-*.md and replace the commands/close.md line with an English paraphrase plus a task-id attribution citing the ticket where the verbatim text is preserved.
violations.advisory_note: Pre-existing in HEAD and HEAD~1; not introduced by task 20260525-050824 diff. Under AC-2.6(b) this finding is advisory/non-blocking for this cycle.

observations.standard_2_generated_tests: OBSERVATION_ONLY — advisory portability debt: tests/generated/20260525-050824/test_AC*.py files all hardcode repo = "/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude". Standard 2 formally targets scripts/ files; test-writer-generated test bodies in tests/generated/ are outside formal Standard 2 scope per prior close precedent. The pre-existing 20260522-000000/test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py instance is introduced_in_diff:false. Concrete fix for next cycle: use `from pathlib import Path; REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]` instead of hardcoded path.
observations.standard_1: CLEAN — no inline bash/python implementation logic in any command or agent .md file in the changed-files set. hooks/doc_sync/tree.py and regen_readme.py are Python modules, not .md files.
observations.standard_2_scripts: CLEAN — score-update.sh, score-inject.sh, and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh all use ${HOME}/.claude/... defaults with --lifecycle-file/--scores-file CLI override parameters. No hardcoded env-specific values.
observations.standard_3_scripts: CLEAN — all three scripts use `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - ... )` with venv co-located on the same line as python3 per spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 2 P3.6. tests/score-inject-contract/test-inject-branches.sh contains no python3 calls. test_AC6 Python subprocess command string includes venv activation and is in a .py file (outside Standard 3 .sh/.md scope).
observations.standard_4: CLEAN — no .md files in the changed-files set. No decimal or lettered step numbering observed.
observations.standard_5: CLEAN — score-update.sh, score-inject.sh, lifecycle-baseline-import.sh use descriptive names. No forbidden patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, etc.).
observations.standard_6_scan_result: Unconditional scan of agents/*.md and commands/*.md found 4 genuine pre-existing CJK violations (qa.md:1297, dev.md:497, ba.md:777, close.md:435). All are introduced_in_diff:false. No new CJK characters in any changed file.
observations.standard_7: CLEAN — score-update.sh (write path), score-inject.sh (read path), and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh (one-time migration) serve distinct purposes and cannot be reasonably merged. No duplicate functionality detected.
observations.standard_8: CLEAN — no .md command or agent files in the changed-files set. Hook files (tree.py, regen_readme.py) are Python modules without documentation prose. Test scripts are concise and functional.
observations.standard_9: CLEAN — same assessment as Standard 2. All script parameters are fully parameterized.
observations.standard_10: CLEAN — commands/close.md and commands/dev.md reference ~/.claude/scripts/score-update.sh, ~/.claude/scripts/score-inject.sh, and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh; all three files exist on disk. test_AC6 references tests/generated/20260522-000000/test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py which exists and has been updated in this cycle.
observations.standard_11: CLEAN — detect-merge-conflicts.sh confirms no merge conflict markers in any changed file. (Conflict markers exist in INDEX.md and docs/dev/ files but those are out of the changed-files scope.)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed Finding #1 (Standard 3 bare python3 at lines 151 and 194 of test-lifecycle-cas.sh) as a genuine critical violation with a proposed fix. Finding #2 (hardcoded repo path in generated tests) was downgraded to OBSERVATION_ONLY advisory — Standard 2 formally targets scripts/, not tests/generated/, per prior close precedent. Finding #3 (Standard 6 CJK hits) was confirmed as genuine violations but clarified as advisory/non-blocking for this cycle (all introduced_in_diff:false). No missed violations found in hook files or generated test files.

findings.issue: Finding #1 (Standard 3 bare python3 at lines 151/194) confirmed as genuine violation
findings.proposed_fix: Replace bare `python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'...PYEOF` with venv-wrapped form: `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'...PYEOF )`
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed the bare python3 calls in test-lifecycle-cas.sh are genuine Standard 3 violations. Violations retained in violations[] with introduced_in_diff:true.

findings.issue: Finding #2 (hardcoded /dev/shm repo path in test_AC*.py) should be advisory/non-blocking, not a formal Standard 2 violation. Standard 2 formally targets scripts/ files; tests/generated/ is outside formal Standard 2 scope per prior close precedent.
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to OBSERVATION_ONLY advisory portability debt. Concrete fix: use `from pathlib import Path; REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]` instead of hardcoded path. Mark 20260522 test_AC4 instance introduced_in_diff:false.
findings.rationale: Finding #2 has been moved to observations section as advisory portability debt rather than a formal violation. Standard 2 violation classification rejected per Codex's scope analysis.

findings.issue: Finding #3 (Standard 6 CJK hits) are genuine non-exempt violations, but the original wording called them 'code comments' — Codex clarifies they are 'prompt prose', not code comments in the technical sense
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'Standard 6 unconditional scan found four genuine pre-existing CJK violations. All are introduced_in_diff:false / blocking_for_close:false under AC-2.6(b). The agent hits are in prompt prose paragraphs.'
findings.rationale: Wording updated in violations[] to accurately describe the location as 'agent prompt' prose, not 'code comments'. The blocking_for_close:false and introduced_in_diff:false fields are explicit in the violations.

findings.issue: No missed violations in hooks/doc_sync/tree.py or hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py; the lifecycle.jsonl/lifecycle.jsonl.lock skip-list additions are intentional required changes.
findings.rationale: Hook files confirmed clean by Codex. No action required.

findings.issue: No additional Standard 3 violations in the new generated test_AC*.py files. test_AC6's python3 reference is inside a Python subprocess command string with venv activation included, and the file is a .py file outside Standard 3 scope.
findings.rationale: Generated test files confirmed clean by Codex. No action required.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding #1 retained as critical violation (introduced_in_diff:true). Finding #2 downgraded from formal Standard 2 violation to advisory observation per Codex scope analysis. Finding #3 wording updated to accurately describe violations as 'agent prompt prose' rather than 'code comments', and blocking_for_close:false made explicit. Two observation-only Codex findings logged.
